title: FOIA request: FBI case number 192C-DN-46497 (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, and the Privacy Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552a, I hereby request copies of all records in the custody or control of the Federal Bureau of Investigation — including records held at FBI Headquarters and at the FBI Denver Field Office — pertaining to the following matter:

Primary case: FBI case number 192C-DN-46497, an armed robbery of a Wells Fargo armored vehicle that occurred on or about July 20, 1993, in Denver, Colorado, investigated by the FBI Denver Field Office.

I request all records associated with or cross-referenced to this case number, including but not limited to:

1/ The complete investigative case file for 192C-DN-46497, including all serials, sub-files, and attachments.
2/ All FD-302 investigative interview reports generated in connection with this case, including any photographic lineup or photo array administered to any Wells Fargo employee, driver, or bystander, and the documented results of those procedures.
3/ All FD-36 teletypes, Electronic Communications (ECs), and AIRTEL communications sent to or from FBIHQ or any field office referencing this case number, including any communications between the FBI Denver Field Office and the FBI Los Angeles Field Office.
4/ All FD-430 statistical and summary reports associated with this case.
5/ All surveillance logs, SOG operational records, and target identification documents associated with this case.
6/ All suspect identification records, including photographs, composite sketches, fingerprint analysis results, and physical description summaries for all participants — including any unidentified third participant believed to have been involved in the robbery.
7/ All inter-agency communications between the FBI Denver Field Office and any other federal or local law enforcement agency referencing this case or the July 20, 1993 Wells Fargo robbery.
8/ All records reflecting any connection between this Denver investigation and any investigation conducted by the FBI Los Angeles Field Office, including all cross-index references in which case number 192C-DN-46497 appears in connection with Los Angeles Field Office case numbers 91A-LA-197438, 91A-LA-198172, 91A-LA-205209, 192C-LA-185807, and 192C-LA-195797.
9/ All evidence logs, chain of custody records, and evidence disposition records associated with this case, including any records reflecting the retention, transfer, or destruction of physical or photographic evidence.
10/ All records reflecting the current status, closure, or disposition of this investigation, including any closure communications, referral records, and any records reflecting a reopening of the case after February 28, 1997.
11/ To the extent any responsive records have been destroyed, all destruction authorization forms, evidence management reports, and records documenting the date, authorization, and method of destruction.

Subjects of interest: This request concerns an investigation into the armed robbery of a Wells Fargo armored vehicle in Denver, Colorado, on or about July 20, 1993. FBI records already in my possession from the Los Angeles Field Office indicate that this case was investigated in connection with Emil Decebal Matasareanu (DOB approximately July 19, 1966; SSN 571-45-4679; birthplace Timisoara, Romania) and Larry Eugene Phillips Jr. (DOB September 20, 1970), as well as at least one additional unidentified individual believed to have participated in the robbery. Both named individuals are deceased as of February 28, 1997. No Privacy Act exemption applies to either.

If the FBI processes this request in installments, I request that the following records be prioritized in the first release: all FD-302 interview reports, all photographic lineup records and their results, all teletypes and Electronic Communications between the Denver Field Office and the FBI Los Angeles Field Office, and all records identifying or describing participants beyond the two named subjects above.

I request that all responsive records be processed for maximum disclosure consistent with FOIA, and that any withholdings be identified with specificity. I request that Exemption 7(C) be applied only as to living, uncharged individuals, and note that the two primary named subjects are deceased.

I am willing to pay reasonable duplication fees. If fees are anticipated to exceed $100.00, please contact me before processing. I request a fee waiver on the grounds that disclosure of these records contributes significantly to public understanding of law enforcement operations and is not primarily in my commercial interest.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown
